metadata.description: Short name for the azd environment, for example dev or prod.
Azure region for the deployment.
Base prefix used for Azure resource names.
Optional override for the resource group name.
Tags applied to every provisioned resource.
Static Web Apps plan. Standard is required for linked backends.
Region for Azure Static Web Apps. This resource type is not available in every Azure region.
Admin username for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
Admin password for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
Read-only PostgreSQL user used by the API at runtime.
Password for the read-only PostgreSQL API user.
Database name used by the application.
PostgreSQL version.
PostgreSQL compute SKU name.
PostgreSQL compute tier.
Storage size for PostgreSQL in GB.
Whether PostgreSQL should expose a public endpoint. Disabled — private networking handles connectivity.
Whether to create the broad Allow Azure Services firewall rule for PostgreSQL. Disabled — private networking handles connectivity.
CPU allocation for the API container app.
Memory allocation for the API container app.
Container port exposed by the Plumber API.
Minimum API replicas.
Maximum API replicas. Keep at 1 unless a distributed rate-limiter is added — the in-process limiter is per-replica.
Optional additional frontend hostname to permit through CORS.
Optional mode for staged private PostgreSQL networking. Set to enabled to provision a VNet-integrated Container Apps environment and private PostgreSQL network.
Optional override for the VNet CIDR used when private PostgreSQL networking is enabled.
Optional override for the Container Apps infrastructure subnet CIDR used when private PostgreSQL networking is enabled.
Optional override for the delegated PostgreSQL subnet CIDR used when private PostgreSQL networking is enabled.
Optional override for the private DNS zone used when private PostgreSQL networking is enabled.
Container image for the API and DB refresh jobs. Populated by azd after first deploy via SERVICE_API_IMAGE_NAME. Empty on first provision — Bicep defaults to the placeholder image.
Short name for the azd environment, for example dev or prod.
Azure region for the deployment.
Base prefix used for Azure resource names.
Tags applied to every provisioned resource.
Static Web Apps plan. Standard is required for linked backends.
Region for Azure Static Web Apps. This can differ from the rest of the stack.
Container image for the API and DB refresh jobs. Defaults to the placeholder; overridden by SERVICE_API_IMAGE_NAME after first deploy.
Admin username for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
Admin password for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
Read-only PostgreSQL user used by the API at runtime.
Password for the read-only PostgreSQL API user.
Database name used by the application.
PostgreSQL version.
PostgreSQL compute SKU name.
PostgreSQL compute tier.
Storage size for PostgreSQL in GB.

Whether PostgreSQL should expose a public endpoint. For production deployments, set privatePostgresNetworkingMode to enabled instead of enabling the public endpoint. See AGENTS.md for the private networking migration guide.

Whether to create the broad Allow Azure Services firewall rule for PostgreSQL. This rule permits any Azure-hosted resource (in any subscription) to connect. Disabled by default; enable only as a temporary stopgap when private networking is not yet configured.
Optional mode for staged private PostgreSQL networking. Set to enabled to provision a VNet-integrated Container Apps environment and private PostgreSQL network.
Optional override for the VNet CIDR used when private PostgreSQL networking is enabled.
Optional override for the Container Apps infrastructure subnet CIDR used when private PostgreSQL networking is enabled.
Optional override for the delegated PostgreSQL subnet CIDR used when private PostgreSQL networking is enabled.
Optional override for the private DNS zone used when private PostgreSQL networking is enabled.
CPU allocation for the API container app.
Memory allocation for the API container app.
Container port exposed by the Plumber API.
Minimum API replicas.

Maximum API replicas. Single replica is recommended for this read-only stats site: the in-process rate limiter is not shared across replicas, so a higher count allows bypass. Increase only after adding a distributed rate store (e.g. Redis).
Optional additional frontend hostname to permit through CORS.

resources.name: [variables('containerEnvironmentName')]
resources.properties: [union(createObject('appLogsConfiguration', createObject('destination', 'log-analytics', 'logAnalyticsConfiguration', createObject('customerId', reference(resourceId('Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces', variables('workspaceName')), '2025-07-01').customerId, 'sharedKey', listKeys(resourceId('Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces', variables('workspaceName')), '2025-07-01').primarySharedKey))), if(variables('enablePrivatePostgresNetworking'), createObject('vnetConfiguration', createObject('infrastructureSubnetId', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets', variables('virtualNetworkName'), variables('containerAppsInfrastructureSubnetName')), 'internal', false()), 'workloadProfiles', createArray(createObject('name', 'Consumption', 'workloadProfileType', 'Consumption'))), createObject()))]
